name: spdx3query
documentation:
# spdx3query
Command line tool for querying SPDX 3 files

## Installation

`spdx3query` can be installed using `pip`:

```shell
python3 -m pip install spdx3query
```

## Usage

`spdx3query` takes one or mode SPDX 3 JSON files as input using the `-i` option
and then a subcommand to query information about it. For example, this command
will list all `Build` objects found in `my-sdpx.spdx.json`:

```shell
spdx3query -i my-spdx.spdx.json find --type build_Build
```

There are many commands that `spdx3query` supports. For a list of commands, see
`spdx3query --help`. Each command also implements a `--help` which can provide
additional information about what it does, for example `spdx3query find --help`

### Interactive mode

In addition to the top level subcommands of `sdpx3query`, there is also an
interactive mode where commands can be run. This can be useful for
interactively exploring SPDX 3 files, particularly if they are very large as
the dataset only needs to be loaded once. To enter interactive mode, the
`interactive` subcommand is used, for example:

```shell
spdx3query -i my-spdx.spdx.json interactive
```

From the prompt, any query subcommand can be run in the same way as if it had
been specified on the command line, for example:

```
> find --type build_Build
```

### Object Mnemonic Handles

Objects in SPDX 3 are often assigned IRIs as identifiers (either in the `@id`
or `spdxId` property, depending on the object). These IRI names are often very
long and can be difficult to type in correctly when performing queries on a
data file. To aid in identifying objects, `spdx3query` assigns a mnemonic to
each device that can be used in place of the identifier. The mnemonic uses
words from the [BIP 39][1] word list, and by default uses 3 terms. If you are
dealing with a large datafile, you can increase the number of terms using the
`--handle-terms` argument to `spdx3query`. The mnemonic handle is based on a
hash of the actual ID, and therefore is stable even when loading the same file
multiple times.

As an example, you can see the mnemonic handle for the following build object
is "chest-acoustic-phone"

```
$ spdx3query -i bitbake.spdx.json find --type build_Build --show
Loaded 18 objects in 0.01s
Found 1 object(s):

build_Build - 'chest-acoustic-phone'
  spdxId: 'http://spdx.org/spdxdoc/bitbake-addba517-4804-5ae3-87c2-0c3a1a5812ba/bitbake/2ae7c23f5bf50e79d5c97b3a3f2294bb'
```

This means that you can can use this mnemonic handle in place of the actual
spdxId to reference this object, e.g.:

```shell
spdx3query -i bitbake.spdx.json show chest-acoustic-phone
```

For objects that do not have an ID, a mnemonic handle will also be assigned,
but it will have a `LOCAL-` prefix prepended to it. These handles are not
guaranteed to remain the same between different invocations of `spdx3query`.
For example:

```
$ spdx3query -i bitbake.spdx.json find --type CreationInfo
Loaded 18 objects in 0.01s
Found 1 object(s):
CreationInfo - 'LOCAL-stereo-window-riot'
```

## Development

Development on `spdx3query` can be done by setting up a virtual environment and
installing it in editable mode:

```shell
python3 -m venv .venv
. .venv/bin/activate
pip install -e .[dev]
```

Tests can be run using pytest:

```shell
pytest -v
```

[1]: https://github.com/bitcoin/bips/blob/master/bip-0039.mediawiki

documentation: Print object tree

functions.name: spdx3query.spdx3.main
documentation: fmt: on

classes.name: spdx3query.spdx3.Property
documentation:
A generic SHACL object property. The different types will derive from this
class

classes.name: spdx3query.spdx3.StringProp
documentation: A scalar string property for an SHACL object

classes.name: spdx3query.spdx3.DateTimeProp
documentation: A Date/Time Object with optional timezone

classes.name: spdx3query.spdx3.DateTimeStampProp
documentation: A Date/Time Object with required timestamp

classes.name: spdx3query.spdx3.ObjectProp
documentation: A scalar SHACL object property of a SHACL object

classes.name: spdx3query.spdx3.ListProp
documentation: A list of SHACL properties

methods.name: spdx3query.spdx3.SHACLObject.walk
spdx3query.spdx3.SHACLObject.iter_objects
documentation: Iterate of all objects that are a child of this one

methods.name: spdx3query.spdx3.SHACLObjectSet.create_index
documentation:
(re)Create object index

Creates or recreates the indices for the object set to enable fast
lookup. All objects and their children are walked and indexed

methods.name: spdx3query.spdx3.SHACLObjectSet.add_index
documentation:
Add object to index

Adds the object to all appropriate indices

methods.name: spdx3query.spdx3.SHACLObjectSet.add
documentation:
Add object to object set

Adds a SHACLObject to the object set and index it.

NOTE: Child objects of the attached object are not indexes

methods.name: spdx3query.spdx3.SHACLObjectSet.update
documentation: Update object set adding all objects in each other iterable

methods.name: spdx3query.spdx3.SHACLObjectSet.__contains__
documentation: Returns True if the item is in the object set

methods.name: spdx3query.spdx3.SHACLObjectSet.link
documentation:
Link object set

Links the object in the object set by replacing string object
references with references to the objects themselves. e.g.
a property that references object "https://foo/bar" by a string
reference will be replaced with an actual reference to the object in
the object set with the same ID if it exists in the object set

If multiple objects with the same ID are found, the duplicates are
eliminated

methods.name: spdx3query.spdx3.SHACLObjectSet.find_by_id
documentation:
Find object by ID

Returns objects that match the specified ID, or default if there is no
object with the specified ID

methods.name: spdx3query.spdx3.SHACLObjectSet.foreach
documentation: Iterate over every object in the object set, and all child objects

methods.name: spdx3query.spdx3.SHACLObjectSet.foreach_type
documentation:
Iterate over each object of a specified type (or subclass there of)

If match_subclass is True, and class derived from typ will also match
(similar to isinstance()). If False, only exact matches will be
returned

methods.name: spdx3query.spdx3.SHACLObjectSet.merge
documentation:
Merge object sets

Returns a new object set that is the combination of this object set and
all provided arguments

methods.name: spdx3query.spdx3.SHACLObjectSet.encode
documentation:
Serialize a list of objects to a serialization encoder

If force_list is true, a list will always be written using the encoder.

methods.name: spdx3query.spdx3.Decoder.read_value
documentation:
Consume next item

Consumes the next item of any type

methods.name: spdx3query.spdx3.Decoder.read_string
documentation:
Consume the next item as a string.

Returns the string value of the next item, or `None` if the next item
is not a string

methods.name: spdx3query.spdx3.Decoder.read_datetime
documentation:
Consumes the next item as a date & time string

Returns the string value of the next item, if it is a ISO datetime, or
`None` if the next item is not a ISO datetime string.

Note that validation of the string is done by the caller, so a minimal
implementation can just check if the next item is a string without
worrying about the format

methods.name: spdx3query.spdx3.Decoder.read_integer
documentation:
Consumes the next item as an integer

Returns the integer value of the next item, or `None` if the next item
is not an integer

methods.name: spdx3query.spdx3.Decoder.read_iri
documentation:
Consumes the next item as an IRI string

Returns the string value of the next item an IRI, or `None` if the next
item is not an IRI.

The returned string should be either a fully-qualified IRI, or a blank
node ID

methods.name: spdx3query.spdx3.Decoder.read_enum
documentation:
Consumes the next item as an Enum value string

Returns the fully qualified IRI of the next enum item, or `None` if the
next item is not an enum value.

The callee is responsible for validating that the returned IRI is
actually a member of the specified Enum, so the `Decoder` does not need
to check that, but can if it wishes

methods.name: spdx3query.spdx3.Decoder.read_bool
documentation:
Consume the next item as a boolean value

Returns the boolean value of the next item, or `None` if the next item
is not a boolean

methods.name: spdx3query.spdx3.Decoder.read_float
documentation:
Consume the next item as a float value

Returns the float value of the next item, or `None` if the next item is
not a float

methods.name: spdx3query.spdx3.Decoder.read_list
documentation:
Consume the next item as a list generator

This should generate a `Decoder` object for each item in the list. The
generated `Decoder` can be used to read the corresponding item from the
list

methods.name: spdx3query.spdx3.Decoder.read_object
documentation:
Consume next item as an object

A context manager that "enters" the next item as a object and yields a
`Decoder` that can read properties from it. If the next item is not an
object, yields `None`

Properties will be read out of the object using `read_property` and
`read_object_id`

methods.name: spdx3query.spdx3.Decoder.read_property
documentation:
Read property from object

A context manager that yields a `Decoder` that can be used to read the
value of the property with the given key in current object, or `None`
if the property does not exist in the current object.

methods.name: spdx3query.spdx3.Decoder.object_keys
documentation:
Read property keys from an object

Iterates over all the serialized keys for the current object

methods.name: spdx3query.spdx3.Decoder.read_object_id
documentation:
Read current object ID property

Returns the ID of the current object if one is defined, or `None` if
the current object has no ID.

The ID must be a fully qualified IRI or a blank node

If `alias` is provided, is is a hint as to another name by which the ID
might be found, if the `Decoder` supports aliases for an ID

methods.name: spdx3query.spdx3.Encoder.write_string
documentation:
Write a string value

Encodes the value as a string in the output

methods.name: spdx3query.spdx3.Encoder.write_datetime
documentation:
Write a date & time string

Encodes the value as an ISO datetime string

Note: The provided string is already correctly encoded as an ISO datetime

methods.name: spdx3query.spdx3.Encoder.write_integer
documentation:
Write an integer value

Encodes the value as an integer in the output

methods.name: spdx3query.spdx3.Encoder.write_iri
documentation:
Write IRI

Encodes the string as an IRI. Note that the string will be either a
fully qualified IRI or a blank node ID. If `compact` is provided and
the serialization supports compacted IRIs, it should be preferred to
the full IRI

methods.name: spdx3query.spdx3.Encoder.write_enum
documentation:
Write enum value IRI

Encodes the string enum value IRI. Note that the string will be a fully
qualified IRI. If `compact` is provided and the serialization supports
compacted IRIs, it should be preferred to the full IRI.

methods.name: spdx3query.spdx3.Encoder.write_bool
documentation:
Write boolean

Encodes the value as a boolean in the output

methods.name: spdx3query.spdx3.Encoder.write_float
documentation:
Write float

Encodes the value as a floating point number in the output

methods.name: spdx3query.spdx3.Encoder.write_object
documentation:
Write object

A context manager that yields an `Encoder` that can be used to encode
the given object properties.

The provided ID will always be a valid ID (even if o._id is `None`), in
case the `Encoder` _must_ have an ID. `needs_id` is a hint to indicate
to the `Encoder` if an ID must be written or not (if that is even an
option). If it is `True`, the `Encoder` must encode an ID for the
object. If `False`, the encoder is not required to encode an ID and may
omit it.

The ID will be either a fully qualified IRI, or a blank node IRI.

Properties will be written the object using `write_property`

methods.name: spdx3query.spdx3.Encoder.write_property
documentation:
Write object property

A context manager that yields an `Encoder` that can be used to encode
the value for the property with the given IRI in the current object

Note that the IRI will be fully qualified. If `compact` is provided and
the serialization supports compacted IRIs, it should be preferred to
the full IRI.

methods.name: spdx3query.spdx3.Encoder.write_list
documentation:
Write list

A context manager that yields an `Encoder` that can be used to encode a
list.

Each item of the list will be added using `write_list_item`

methods.name: spdx3query.spdx3.Encoder.write_list_item
documentation:
Write list item

A context manager that yields an `Encoder` that can be used to encode
the value for a list item

methods.name: spdx3query.spdx3.JSONLDSerializer.write
documentation:
Write a SHACLObjectSet to a JSON LD file

If force_at_graph is True, a @graph node will always be written

methods.name: spdx3query.spdx3.JSONLDInlineSerializer.write
documentation:
Write a SHACLObjectSet to a JSON LD file

Note: force_at_graph is included for compatibility, but ignored. This
serializer always writes out a graph
